name: Pulled Ham and Egg Sandwich
servings: serves 8 | prep. time 20 minutes | cooking time 7-9 hours

ingredients.item: butter, more for spreading, at room temperature
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste

method: Add ham, mustard, maple syrup, beer, ground pepper, and rosemary to a slow cooker. Stir to combine. Cover and cook on LOW for 7-9 hours.
Transfer the ham to a plate and let it cool. Once cool, shred the meat and remove the bone. Keep the bone for a soup base.
Remove the rosemary sprigs and skim the fat.
Place the shredded pork back into the slow cooker.
Let the butter melt in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. In batches, fry the eggs sunny-side-up until cooked to the desired doneness. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Set aside.
Simultaneously, toast the buns and spread with butter on both sides.
Lay a few lettuce leaves on the bottom buns and add some butter pickle slices if desired.
Top with a generous amount of shredded ham, a Swiss cheese slice, and top with a fried egg. Top with remaining buns and serve immediately.
